NKJV.1: Then as He went out of the temple, one of His disciples said to Him, “Teacher, see what manner of stones and what buildings are here!”
NKJV.2: And Jesus answered and said to him, “Do you see these great buildings? Not one stone shall be left upon another, that shall not be thrown down.”
NKJV.3: Now as He sat on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked Him privately,
NKJV.4: “Tell us, when will these things be? And what will be the sign when all these things will be fulfilled?”
NKJV.5: And Jesus, answering them, began to say: “Take heed that no one deceives you.
NKJV.6: For many will come in My name, saying, ‘I am He,’ and will deceive many.
NKJV.7: But when you hear of wars and rumors of wars, do not be troubled; for such things must happen, but the end is not yet.
NKJV.8: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. And there will be earthquakes in various places, and there will be famines and troubles. These are the beginnings of sorrows.
NKJV.9: “But  watch out for yourselves, for they will deliver you up to councils, and you will be beaten in the synagogues. You will be brought before rulers and kings for My sake, for a testimony to them.
NKJV.10: And  the gospel must first be preached to all the nations.
NKJV.11: But when they arrest you and deliver you up, do not worry beforehand, or premeditate what you will speak. But whatever is given you in that hour, speak that; for it is not you who speak, but the Holy Spirit.
NKJV.12: Now brother will betray brother to death, and a father his child; and children will rise up against parents and cause them to be put to death.
NKJV.13: And you will be hated by all for My name’s sake. But  he who endures to the end shall be saved.
NKJV.14: “So when you see the  ‘abomination of desolation,’ spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing where it ought not” (let the reader understand), “then  let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.
NKJV.15: Let him who is on the housetop not go down into the house, nor enter to take anything out of his house.
NKJV.16: And let him who is in the field not go back to get his clothes.
NKJV.17: But woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing babies in those days!
NKJV.18: And pray that your flight may not be in winter.
NKJV.19: For in those days there will be tribulation, such as has not been since the beginning of the creation which God created until this time, nor ever shall be.
NKJV.20: And unless the Lord had shortened those days, no flesh would be saved; but for the elect’s sake, whom He chose, He shortened the days.
NKJV.21: “Then if anyone says to you, ‘Look, here is the Christ!’ or, ‘Look, He is there!’ do not believe it.
NKJV.22: For false christs and false prophets will rise and show signs and  wonders to deceive, if possible, even the elect.
NKJV.23: But  take heed; see, I have told you all things beforehand.
NKJV.24: “But in those days, after that tribulation, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light;
NKJV.25: the stars of heaven will fall, and the powers in the heavens will be  shaken.
NKJV.26: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in the clouds with great power and glory.
NKJV.27: And then He will send His angels, and gather together His elect from the four winds, from the farthest part of earth to the farthest part of heaven.
NKJV.28: “Now learn this parable from the fig tree: When its branch has already become tender, and puts forth leaves, you know that summer is near.
NKJV.29: So you also, when you see these things happening, know that it is near—at the doors!
NKJV.30: Assuredly, I say to you, this generation will by no means pass away till all these things take place.
NKJV.31: Heaven and earth will pass away, but  My words will by no means pass away.
NKJV.32: “But of that day and hour  no one knows, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the  Father.
NKJV.33: Take heed, watch and pray; for you do not know when the time is.
NKJV.34: It is like a man going to a far country, who left his house and gave authority to his servants, and to each his work, and commanded the doorkeeper to watch.
NKJV.35: Watch therefore, for you do not know when the master of the house is coming—in the evening, at midnight, at the crowing of the rooster, or in the morning—
NKJV.36: lest, coming suddenly, he find you sleeping.
NKJV.37: And what I say to you, I say to all: Watch!”

ESV.1: And as he came out of the temple, one of his disciples said to him, “Look, Teacher, what wonderful stones and what wonderful buildings!”
ESV.2: And Jesus said to him, “Do you see these great buildings? There will not be left here one stone upon another that will not be thrown down.”
ESV.3: And as he sat on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter and James and John and Andrew asked him privately,
ESV.4: “Tell us, when will these things be, and what will be the sign when all these things are about to be accomplished?”
ESV.5: And Jesus began to say to them,  “See that no one leads you astray.
ESV.6: Many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he!’ and they will lead many astray.
ESV.7: And when you hear of wars and rumors of wars, do not be alarmed. This must take place, but the end is not yet.
ESV.8: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places; there will be famines. These are but the beginning of the birth pains.
ESV.9: “But be on your guard. For they will deliver you over to councils, and you will be beaten in synagogues, and you will stand before governors and kings for my sake, to bear witness before them.
ESV.10: And the gospel must first be proclaimed to all nations.
ESV.11: And when they bring you to trial and deliver you over, do not be anxious beforehand what you are to say, but say whatever is given you in that hour, for it is not you who speak, but the Holy Spirit.
ESV.12: And brother will deliver brother over to death, and the father his child, and children will rise against parents and have them put to death.
ESV.13: And you will be hated by all for my name's sake. But the one who endures to the end will be saved.
ESV.14: “But when you see the abomination of desolation standing where he ought not to be (let the reader understand), then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.
ESV.15: Let the one who is on the housetop not go down, nor enter his house, to take anything out,
ESV.16: and let the one who is in the field not turn back to take his cloak.
ESV.17: And alas for women who are pregnant and for those who are nursing infants in those days!
ESV.18: Pray that it may not happen in winter.
ESV.19: For in those days there will be such tribulation as has not been from the beginning of the creation that God created until now, and never will be.
ESV.20: And if the Lord had not cut short the days, no human being would be saved. But for the sake of the elect, whom he chose, he shortened the days.
ESV.21: And then if anyone says to you, ‘Look, here is the Christ!’ or ‘Look, there he is!’ do not believe it.
ESV.22: For false christs and false prophets will arise and perform signs and wonders, to lead astray, if possible, the elect.
ESV.23: But be on guard; I have told you all things beforehand.
ESV.24: “But in those days, after that tribulation, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light,
ESV.25: and the stars will be falling from heaven, and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.
ESV.26: And then they will see the Son of Man coming in clouds with great power and glory.
ESV.27: And then he will send out the angels and gather his elect from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of heaven.
ESV.28: “From the fig tree learn its lesson: as soon as its branch becomes tender and puts out its leaves, you know that summer is near.
ESV.29: So also, when you see these things taking place, you know that he is near, at the very gates.
ESV.30: Truly, I say to you, this generation will not pass away until all these things take place.
ESV.31: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
ESV.32: “But concerning that day or that hour, no one knows, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
ESV.33: Be on guard, keep awake.  For you do not know when the time will come.
ESV.34: It is like a man going on a journey, when he leaves home and puts his servants  in charge, each with his work, and commands the doorkeeper to stay awake.
ESV.35: Therefore stay awake — for you do not know when the master of the house will come, in the evening, or at midnight, or when the rooster crows,  or in the morning —
ESV.36: lest he come suddenly and find you asleep.
ESV.37: And what I say to you I say to all: Stay awake.”

NASB.1: As He was going out of the temple, one of His disciples said to Him, 'Teacher, behold what wonderful stones and what wonderful buildings!'
NASB.2: And Jesus said to him, 'Do you see these great buildings? Not one stone will be left upon another which will not be torn down.'
NASB.3: As He was sitting on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter and James and John and Andrew were questioning Him privately,
NASB.4: 'Tell us, when will these things be, and what [will be] the sign when all these things are going to be fulfilled?'
NASB.5: And Jesus began to say to them, 'See to it that no one misleads you.
NASB.6: 'Many will come in My name, saying, 'I am [He!]' and will mislead many.
NASB.7: 'When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, do not be frightened; [those things] must take place; but [that is] not yet the end.
NASB.8: 'For nation will rise up against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; there will be earthquakes in various places; there will [also] be famines. These things are [merely] the beginning of birth pangs.
NASB.9: 'But be on your guard; for they will deliver you to [the] courts, and you will be flogged in [the] synagogues, and you will stand before governors and kings for My sake, as a testimony to them.
NASB.10: 'The gospel must first be preached to all the nations.
NASB.11: 'When they arrest you and hand you over, do not worry beforehand about what you are to say, but say whatever is given you in that hour; for it is not you who speak, but [it is] the Holy Spirit.
NASB.12: 'Brother will betray brother to death, and a father [his] child; and children will rise up against parents and have them put to death.
NASB.13: 'You will be hated by all because of My name, but the one who endures to the end, he will be saved.
NASB.14: 'But when you see the ABOMINATION OF DESOLATION standing where it should not be (let the reader understand), then those who are in Judea must flee to the mountains.
NASB.15: 'The one who is on the housetop must not go down, or go in to get anything out of his house;
NASB.16: and the one who is in the field must not turn back to get his coat.
NASB.17: 'But woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing babies in those days!
NASB.18: 'But pray that it may not happen in the winter.
NASB.19: 'For those days will be a [time of] tribulation such as has not occurred since the beginning of the creation which God created until now, and never will.
NASB.20: 'Unless the Lord had shortened [those] days, no life would have been saved; but for the sake of the elect, whom He chose, He shortened the days.
NASB.21: 'And then if anyone says to you, 'Behold, here is the Christ'; or, 'Behold, [He is] there'; do not believe [him];
NASB.22: for false Christs and false prophets will arise, and will show signs and wonders, in order to lead astray, if possible, the elect.
NASB.23: 'But take heed; behold, I have told you everything in advance.
NASB.24: 'But in those days, after that tribulation, THE SUN WILL BE DARKENED AND THE MOON WILL NOT GIVE ITS LIGHT,
NASB.25: AND THE STARS WILL BE FALLING from heaven, and the powers that are in the heavens will be shaken.
NASB.26: 'Then they will see THE SON OF MAN COMING IN CLOUDS with great power and glory.
NASB.27: 'And then He will send forth the angels, and will gather together His elect from the four winds, from the farthest end of the earth to the farthest end of heaven.
NASB.28: 'Now learn the parable from the fig tree: when its branch has already become tender and puts forth its leaves, you know that summer is near.
NASB.29: 'Even so, you too, when you see these things happening, recognize that He is near, [right] at the door.
NASB.30: 'Truly I say to you, this generation will not pass away until all these things take place.
NASB.31: 'Heaven and earth will pass away, but My words will not pass away.
NASB.32: 'But of that day or hour no one knows, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but the Father [alone].
NASB.33: 'Take heed, keep on the alert; for you do not know when the [appointed] time will come.
NASB.34: '[It is] like a man away on a journey, [who] upon leaving his house and putting his slaves in charge, [assigning] to each one his task, also commanded the doorkeeper to stay on the alert.
NASB.35: 'Therefore, be on the alert-- for you do not know when the master of the house is coming, whether in the evening, at midnight, or when the rooster crows, or in the morning--
NASB.36: in case he should come suddenly and find you asleep.
NASB.37: 'What I say to you I say to all, 'Be on the alert!''

NLT.1: As Jesus was leaving the Temple that day, one of his disciples said, “Teacher, look at these magnificent buildings! Look at the impressive stones in the walls.”
NLT.2: Jesus replied, “Yes, look at these great buildings. But they will be completely demolished. Not one stone will be left on top of another!”
NLT.3: Later, Jesus sat on the Mount of Olives across the valley from the Temple. Peter, James, John, and Andrew came to him privately and asked him,
NLT.4: “Tell us, when will all this happen? What sign will show us that these things are about to be fulfilled?”
NLT.5: Jesus replied, “Don’t let anyone mislead you,
NLT.6: for many will come in my name, claiming, ‘I am the Messiah.’ They will deceive many.
NLT.7: And you will hear of wars and threats of wars, but don’t panic. Yes, these things must take place, but the end won’t follow immediately.
NLT.8: Nation will go to war against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in many parts of the world, as well as famines. But this is only the first of the birth pains, with more to come.
NLT.9: “When these things begin to happen, watch out! You will be handed over to the local councils and beaten in the synagogues. You will stand trial before governors and kings because you are my followers. But this will be your opportunity to tell them about me.
NLT.10: For the Good News must first be preached to all nations.
NLT.11: But when you are arrested and stand trial, don’t worry in advance about what to say. Just say what God tells you at that time, for it is not you who will be speaking, but the Holy Spirit.
NLT.12: “A brother will betray his brother to death, a father will betray his own child, and children will rebel against their parents and cause them to be killed.
NLT.13: And everyone will hate you because you are my followers. But the one who endures to the end will be saved.
NLT.14: “The day is coming when you will see the sacrilegious object that causes desecration standing where he should not be.” (Reader, pay attention!) “Then those in Judea must flee to the hills.
NLT.15: A person out on the deck of a roof must not go down into the house to pack.
NLT.16: A person out in the field must not return even to get a coat.
NLT.17: How terrible it will be for pregnant women and for nursing mothers in those days.
NLT.18: And pray that your flight will not be in winter.
NLT.19: For there will be greater anguish in those days than at any time since God created the world. And it will never be so great again.
NLT.20: In fact, unless the Lord shortens that time of calamity, not a single person will survive. But for the sake of his chosen ones he has shortened those days.
NLT.21: “Then if anyone tells you, ‘Look, here is the Messiah,’ or ‘There he is,’ don’t believe it.
NLT.22: For false messiahs and false prophets will rise up and perform signs and wonders so as to deceive, if possible, even God’s chosen ones.
NLT.23: Watch out! I have warned you about this ahead of time!
NLT.24:
“At that time, after the anguish of those days, the sun will be darkened,
the moon will give no light,
NLT.25:
the stars will fall from the sky,
and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.
NLT.26: Then everyone will see the Son of Man coming on the clouds with great power and glory.
NLT.27: And he will send out his angels to gather his chosen ones from all over the world — from the farthest ends of the earth and heaven.
NLT.28: “Now learn a lesson from the fig tree. When its branches bud and its leaves begin to sprout, you know that summer is near.
NLT.29: In the same way, when you see all these things taking place, you can know that his return is very near, right at the door.
NLT.30: I tell you the truth, this generation will not pass from the scene before all these things take place.
NLT.31: Heaven and earth will disappear, but my words will never disappear.
NLT.32: “However, no one knows the day or hour when these things will happen, not even the angels in heaven or the Son himself. Only the Father knows.
NLT.33: And since you don’t know when that time will come, be on guard! Stay alert !
NLT.34: “The coming of the Son of Man can be illustrated by the story of a man going on a long trip. When he left home, he gave each of his slaves instructions about the work they were to do, and he told the gatekeeper to watch for his return.
NLT.35: You, too, must keep watch! For you don’t know when the master of the household will return — in the evening, at midnight, before dawn, or at daybreak.
NLT.36: Don’t let him find you sleeping when he arrives without warning.
NLT.37: I say to you what I say to everyone: Watch for him!”

WEB.1: As he went out of the temple, one of his disciples said to him, “Teacher, see what kind of stones and what kind of buildings!”
WEB.2: Jesus said to him, “Do you see these great buildings? There will not be left here one stone on another, which will not be thrown down.”
WEB.3: As he sat on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately,
WEB.4: “Tell us, when will these things be? What is the sign that these things are all about to be fulfilled?”
WEB.5: Jesus, answering, began to tell them, “Be careful that no one leads you astray.
WEB.6: For many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he!’  and will lead many astray.
WEB.7: “When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, don’t be troubled. For those must happen, but the end is not yet.
WEB.8: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places. There will be famines and troubles. These things are the beginning of birth pains.
WEB.9: But watch yourselves, for they will deliver you up to councils. You will be beaten in synagogues. You will stand before rulers and kings for my sake, for a testimony to them.
WEB.10: The Good News must first be preached to all the nations.
WEB.11: When they lead you away and deliver you up, don’t be anxious beforehand, or premeditate what you will say, but say whatever will be given you in that hour. For it is not you who speak, but the Holy Spirit.
WEB.12: “Brother will deliver up brother to death, and the father his child. Children will rise up against parents, and cause them to be put to death.
WEB.13: You will be hated by all men for my name’s sake, but he who endures to the end, the same will be saved.
WEB.14: But when you see the abomination of desolation,  spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing where it ought not (let the reader understand), then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains,
WEB.15: and let him who is on the housetop not go down, nor enter in, to take anything out of his house.
WEB.16: Let him who is in the field not return back to take his cloak.
WEB.17: But woe to those who are with child and to those who nurse babies in those days!
WEB.18: Pray that your flight won’t be in the winter.
WEB.19: For in those days there will be oppression, such as there has not been the like from the beginning of the creation which God created until now, and never will be.
WEB.20: Unless the Lord had shortened the days, no flesh would have been saved; but for the sake of the chosen ones, whom he picked out, he shortened the days.
WEB.21: Then if anyone tells you, ‘Look, here is the Christ!’ or, ‘Look, there!’ don’t believe it.
WEB.22: For there will arise false christs and false prophets, and will show signs and wonders, that they may lead astray, if possible, even the chosen ones.
WEB.23: But you watch.  “Behold, I have told you all things beforehand.
WEB.24: But in those days, after that oppression, the sun will be darkened, the moon will not give its light,
WEB.25: the stars will be falling from the sky, and the powers that are in the heavens will be shaken.
WEB.26: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in clouds with great power and glory.
WEB.27: Then he will send out his angels, and will gather together his chosen ones from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of the sky.
WEB.28: “Now from the fig tree, learn this parable. When the branch has now become tender, and produces its leaves, you know that the summer is near;
WEB.29: even so you also, when you see these things coming to pass, know that it is near, at the doors.
WEB.30: Most certainly I say to you, this generation  will not pass away until all these things happen.
WEB.31: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
WEB.32: But of that day or that hour no one knows, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
WEB.33: Watch, keep alert, and pray; for you don’t know when the time is.
WEB.34: “It is like a man, traveling to another country, having left his house, and given authority to his servants, and to each one his work, and also commanded the doorkeeper to keep watch.
WEB.35: Watch therefore, for you don’t know when the lord of the house is coming, whether at evening, or at midnight, or when the rooster crows, or in the morning;
WEB.36: lest coming suddenly he might find you sleeping.
WEB.37: What I tell you, I tell all: Watch.”

CSB17.1: As <sup>ⓑ</sup> he was going out of the temple, <sup>ⓒ</sup> one of his disciples said to him, “Teacher, <sup>ⓓ</sup> look! What massive stones! What impressive buildings!” <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.2: Jesus said to him, “Do you see these great buildings? <sup>ⓕ</sup> Not one stone will be left upon another — all will be thrown down.”
CSB17.3: While he was sitting on the Mount of Olives <sup>ⓖ</sup> across from the temple, <sup>ⓗ</sup> Peter, <sup>ⓘ</sup> James, <sup>ⓙ</sup> John, <sup>ⓚ</sup> and Andrew <sup>ⓛ</sup> asked him privately,
CSB17.4: “Tell us, when will these things happen? And what will be the sign when all these things are about to be accomplished?”
CSB17.5: Jesus told them, “Watch out that no one deceives you.
CSB17.6: Many will come in my name, <sup>ⓜ</sup> saying, ‘I am he,’ <sup>ⓝ</sup> and they will deceive <sup>ⓐ</sup> many.
CSB17.7: When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, <sup>ⓑ</sup> don’t be alarmed; <sup>ⓒ</sup> these things must take place, <sup>ⓓ</sup> but it is not yet the end.
CSB17.8: For nation will rise up against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. <sup>ⓔ</sup>There will be earthquakes in various places, and famines. <sup>[1]</sup>These are the beginning <sup>ⓕ</sup>of birth pains. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.9: “But you, be on your guard! They will hand you over to local courts, <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓗ</sup> and you will be flogged <sup>ⓘ</sup> in the synagogues. <sup>ⓙ</sup> You will stand before governors and kings <sup>ⓚ</sup> because of me, <sup>ⓛ</sup> as a witness <sup>ⓜ</sup> to them. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.10: And it is necessary that the gospel <sup>ⓐ</sup> be preached to all nations.
CSB17.11: So when they arrest you and hand you over, don’t worry beforehand what you will say, but say whatever is given to you at that time, for it isn’t you speaking, but the Holy Spirit.
CSB17.12: “Brother will betray <sup>ⓑ</sup> brother to death, <sup>ⓒ</sup> and a father his child. Children will rise up against parents and have them put to death.
CSB17.13: You will be hated by everyone because of my name, <sup>ⓓ</sup> but the one who endures to the end will be saved. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.14: “When you see the abomination of desolation [3] ⓕ standing where it should not be” (let the reader understand), “then those in Judea ⓖmust flee to the mountains.
CSB17.15: A man on the housetop must not come down or go in to get anything out of his house, <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.16: and a man in the field must not go back to get his coat. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.17: Woe <sup>ⓙ</sup> to pregnant women and nursing mothers in those days!
CSB17.19: For those will be days of tribulation, <sup>ⓛ</sup> the kind that hasn’t been from the beginning <sup>ⓜ</sup> of creation <sup>ⓝ</sup> until now and never will be again.
CSB17.20: If the Lord <sup>ⓐ</sup> had not cut those days short, <sup>ⓑ</sup> no one would be saved. But he cut those days short for the sake of the elect, <sup>ⓒ</sup> whom he chose. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.21: “Then if anyone tells you, ‘See, here is the Messiah! <sup>ⓔ</sup> See, there! ’ do not believe it.
CSB17.22: For false messiahs and false prophets will arise and will perform signs and wonders <sup>ⓕ</sup> to lead astray, if possible, the elect.
CSB17.23: And you must watch! I have told you everything in advance.
CSB17.24: “But in those days, after that tribulation: <sup>ⓖ</sup> The sun will be darkened, and the moon will not shed its light;
CSB17.25: the stars <sup>ⓗ</sup> will be falling from the sky, and the powers in the heavens will be shaken. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.26: Then they will see the Son of Man <sup>ⓙ</sup> coming <sup>ⓚ</sup> in clouds <sup>ⓛ</sup> with great power <sup>ⓜ</sup> and glory. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.27: He will send out the angels <sup>ⓐ</sup> and gather his elect <sup>ⓑ</sup> from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of heaven. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.28: “Learn <sup>ⓓ</sup> this lesson <sup>ⓔ</sup> from the fig <sup>ⓕ</sup> tree: As soon as its branch becomes tender and sprouts leaves, you know that summer is near. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.29: In the same way, when you see these things happening, recognize <sup>[5]</sup>that he <sup>[6]</sup>is near — at the door.
CSB17.30: “Truly I tell you, <sup>ⓗ</sup> this generation will certainly not pass away until all these things take place.
CSB17.31: Heaven and earth <sup>ⓘ</sup> will pass away, <sup>ⓙ</sup> but my words will never pass away. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.32: “Now concerning that day <sup>ⓛ</sup> or hour no one knows — neither the angels <sup>ⓜ</sup> in heaven nor the Son <sup>ⓝ</sup>  — but only the Father. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.33: “Watch! Be alert! <sup>[7]</sup> For you don’t know when the time <sup>ⓑ</sup> is coming. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.34: “It is like <sup>ⓓ</sup> a man on a journey, who left his house, gave authority to his servants, <sup>ⓔ</sup> gave each one his work, and commanded the doorkeeper to be alert.
CSB17.35: Therefore be alert, <sup>ⓕ</sup> since you don’t know when the master <sup>ⓖ</sup> of the house is coming — whether in the evening or at midnight or at the crowing of the rooster or early in the morning.
CSB17.36: Otherwise, when he comes suddenly he might find you sleeping. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.37: And what I say to you, I say to everyone: Be alert!”

AMP.1: As He was coming out of the temple [grounds], one of His disciples said to Him, "Teacher, look what wonderful stones and what wonderful buildings!"
AMP.2: Jesus replied to him, "You see these great buildings? Not one stone will be left on another which will not be torn down!"
AMP.3: As He was sitting on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter and James and John and Andrew asked Him privately,
AMP.4: "Tell us, when will these things happen, and what will be the sign when all these things are about to be fulfilled?"
AMP.5: Jesus began to say to them, "Be careful and see to it that no one misleads you.
AMP.6: Many will come in My name [misusing My name or claiming to be the Messiah], saying, 'I am He!' and will deceive and mislead many.
AMP.7: When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, do not be alarmed (frightened, troubled); these things must take place, but the end is not yet.
AMP.8: For nation will rise up against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; there will be earthquakes in various places; there will be famines. These things are the beginning of the birth pangs [the intolerable anguish and suffering].
AMP.9: "But be on your guard; they will turn you over to courts, and you will be beaten in synagogues, and you will stand [as accused] before governors and kings for My sake, as a testimony to them.
AMP.10: The gospel [that is, the good news regarding the way of salvation] must first be preached to all the [Gentile] nations.
AMP.11: When they take you and turn you over [to the court], do not worry beforehand about what to say, but say whatever is given to you [by God] in that hour; for it is not you who speak, but it is the Holy Spirit [who will speak through you].
AMP.12: Brother will betray brother to [be put to] death, and a father [will hand over] his child; and children will rise up and take a stand against parents and have them put to death.
AMP.13: You will be hated by everyone because of [your association with] My name, but the one who [patiently perseveres empowered by the Holy Spirit and] endures to the end, he will be saved.
AMP.14: "But when you see theabomination of desolation standing [in the temple sanctuary] where it ought not to be (let the reader understand) then those who are in Judea must flee to the mountains.
AMP.15: Whoever is on the housetop must not go down [to enter the house], or go inside to take anything out of his house;
AMP.16: whoever is in the field must not turn back to get his coat.
AMP.17: And woe to those women who are pregnant and to those who are nursing babies in those days!
AMP.18: Pray that it will not occur in winter,
AMP.19: for at that time there will be such tribulation as has not occurred, from the beginning of the creation which God made, until now—and never will [be again].
AMP.20: And if the Lord had not shortened the days, no human life would have been saved; but for the sake of the elect, whom He chose [for Himself], He shortened the days.
AMP.21: Then if anyone says to you, 'Look, here is the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed)!' or, 'Look, He is there!' do not believe it;
AMP.22: for false Christs and false prophets will arise, and they will provide signs and wonders in order to deceive, if [such a thing were] possible, even the elect [those God has chosen for Himself].
AMP.23: But be on your guard; I have told you everything in advance.
AMP.24: "But in those days, after [the suffering and distress of] that tribulation, the sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light,
AMP.25: <e>and the stars will be falling</e> from the sky, and the powers that are in the heavens will be shaken.
AMP.26: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in clouds with great power and glory [in royal majesty and splendor].
AMP.27: And then He will send out the angels, and will gather together His elect [those He has chosen for Himself] from the four winds, from the farthest end of the earth to the farthest end of heaven.
AMP.28: "Now learn this lesson from the fig tree: as soon as its branch becomes tender and it puts out its leaves, you recognize that summer is near.
AMP.29: Even so, you too, when you see these things happening, know [for certain] that He is near, right at the door.
AMP.30: I assure you and most solemnly say to you, this generation [the people living when these signs and events begin] will not pass away until all these things take place.
AMP.31: Heaven and earth [as now known] will pass away, but My words will not pass away.
AMP.32: But of that [exact] day or hour no one knows, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son [in His humanity], but the Father alone.
AMP.33: "Be on guard and stay constantly alert[and pray]; for you do not know when the appointed time will come.
AMP.34: It is like a man away on a journey, who when he left home put his servants in charge, each with his particular task, and also ordered the doorkeeper to be continually alert.
AMP.35: Therefore, be continually on the alert—for you do not know when the master of the house is coming, whether in the evening, or at midnight, or when the rooster crows, or in the morning—
AMP.36: [stay alert,] in case he should come suddenly and unexpectedly and find you asleep and unprepared.
AMP.37: What I say to you I say to everyone, 'Be on the alert [stay awake and be continually cautious]!'"

MSG.1: As he walked away from the Temple, one of his disciples said, "Teacher, look at that stonework! Those buildings!"
MSG.2: Jesus said, "You're impressed by this grandiose architecture? There's not a stone in the whole works that is not going to end up in a heap of rubble."
MSG.3: Later, as he was sitting on Mount Olives in full view of the Temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew got him off by himself and asked,
MSG.4: "Tell us, when is this going to happen? What sign will we get that things are coming to a head?"
MSG.5: Jesus began, "Watch out for doomsday deceivers.
MSG.6: Many leaders are going to show up with forged identities claiming, 'I'm the One.' They will deceive a lot of people.
MSG.7: When you hear of wars and rumored wars, keep your head and don't panic. This is routine history, and no sign of the end.
MSG.8: Nation will fight nation and ruler fight ruler, over and over. Earthquakes will occur in various places. There will be famines. But these things are nothing compared to what's coming.
MSG.9: "And watch out! They're going to drag you into court. And then it will go from bad to worse, dog-eat-dog, everyone at your throat because you carry my name. You're placed there as sentinels to truth.
MSG.10: The Message has to be preached all across the world.
MSG.11: "When they bring you, betrayed, into court, don't worry about what you'll say. When the time comes, say what's on your heart—the Holy Spirit will make his witness in and through you.
MSG.12: "It's going to be brother killing brother, father killing child, children killing parents.
MSG.13: There's no telling who will hate you because of me. "Stay with it—that's what is required. Stay with it to the end. You won't be sorry; you'll be saved.
MSG.14: "But be ready to run for it when you see the monster of desecration set up where it should never be. You who can read, make sure you understand what I'm talking about. If you're living in Judea at the time, run for the hills;
MSG.15: if you're working in the yard, don't go back to the house to get anything;
MSG.16: if you're out in the field, don't go back to get your coat.
MSG.17: Pregnant and nursing mothers will have it especially hard.
MSG.18: Hope and pray this won't happen in the middle of winter.
MSG.19: "These are going to be hard days—nothing like it from the time God made the world right up to the present. And there'll be nothing like it again.
MSG.20: If he let the days of trouble run their course, nobody would make it. But because of God's chosen people, those he personally chose, he has already intervened.
MSG.21: "If anyone tries to flag you down, calling out, 'Here's the Messiah!' or points, 'There he is!' don't fall for it.
MSG.22: Fake Messiahs and lying preachers are going to pop up everywhere. Their impressive credentials and dazzling performances will pull the wool over the eyes of even those who ought to know better.
MSG.23: So watch out. I've given you fair warning.
MSG.24: "Following those hard times, Sun will fade out, moon cloud over,
MSG.25: Stars fall out of the sky, cosmic powers tremble.
MSG.26: "And then they'll see the Son of Man enter in grand style, his Arrival filling the sky—no one will miss it!
MSG.27: He'll dispatch the angels; they will pull in the chosen from the four winds, from pole to pole.
MSG.28: "Take a lesson from the fig tree. From the moment you notice its buds form, the merest hint of green, you know summer's just around the corner.
MSG.29: And so it is with you. When you see all these things, you know he is at the door.
MSG.30: Don't take this lightly. I'm not just saying this for some future generation, but for this one, too—these things will happen.
MSG.31: Sky and earth will wear out; my words won't wear out.
MSG.32: "But the exact day and hour? No one knows that, not even heaven's angels, not even the Son. Only the Father.
MSG.33: So keep a sharp lookout, for you don't know the timetable.
MSG.34: It's like a man who takes a trip, leaving home and putting his servants in charge, each assigned a task, and commanding the gatekeeper to stand watch.
MSG.35: So, stay at your post, watching. You have no idea when the homeowner is returning, whether evening, midnight, cockcrow, or morning.
MSG.36: You don't want him showing up unannounced, with you asleep on the job.
MSG.37: I say it to you, and I'm saying it to all: Stay at your post. Keep watch."

NIV.1:
Signs of the End of the Age
As he was leaving the temple, one of his disciples said to him, “Look, Teacher! What massive stones! What magnificent buildings!”
NIV.2: “Do you see all these great buildings?” replied Jesus. “Not one stone here will be left on another; every one will be thrown down.”
NIV.3: As Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John and Andrew asked him privately,
NIV.4: “Tell us, when will these things happen? And what will be the sign that they are all about to be fulfilled?”
NIV.5: Jesus said to them: “Watch out that no one deceives you.
NIV.6: Many will come in my name, claiming, ‘I am he,’ and will deceive many.
NIV.7: When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, do not be alarmed. Such things must happen, but the end is still to come.
NIV.8: Nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places, and famines. These are the beginning of birth pains.
NIV.9: “You must be on your guard. You will be handed over to the local councils and flogged in the synagogues. On account of me you will stand before governors and kings as witnesses to them.
NIV.10: And the gospel must first be preached to all nations.
NIV.11: Whenever you are arrested and brought to trial, do not worry beforehand about what to say. Just say whatever is given you at the time, for it is not you speaking, but the Holy Spirit.
NIV.12: “Brother will betray brother to death, and a father his child. Children will rebel against their parents and have them put to death.
NIV.13: All men will hate you because of me, but he who stands firm to the end will be saved.
NIV.14: “When you see ‘the abomination that causes desolation’ standing where it does not belong—let the reader understand—then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.
NIV.15: Let no one on the roof of his house go down or enter the house to take anything out.
NIV.16: Let no one in the field go back to get his cloak.
NIV.17: How dreadful it will be in those days for pregnant women and nursing mothers!
NIV.18: Pray that this will not take place in winter,
NIV.19: because those will be days of distress unequaled from the beginning, when God created the world, until now—and never to be equaled again.
NIV.20: If the Lord had not cut short those days, no one would survive. But for the sake of the elect, whom he has chosen, he has shortened them.
NIV.21: At that time if anyone says to you, ‘Look, here is the Christ!’ or, ‘Look, there he is!’ do not believe it.
NIV.22: For false Christs and false prophets will appear and perform signs and miracles to deceive the elect—if that were possible.
NIV.23: So be on your guard; I have told you everything ahead of time.
NIV.24:
“But in those days, following that distress,
“‘the sun will be darkened,
and the moon will not give its light;
NIV.25:
the stars will fall from the sky,
and the heavenly bodies will be shaken.’
NIV.26: “At that time men will see the Son of Man coming in clouds with great power and glory.
NIV.27: And he will send his angels and gather his elect from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of the heavens.
NIV.28: “Now learn this lesson from the fig tree: As soon as its twigs get tender and its leaves come out, you know that summer is near.
NIV.29: Even so, when you see these things happening, you know that it is near, right at the door.
NIV.30: I tell you the truth, this generation will certainly not pass away until all these things have happened.
NIV.31: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.
NIV.32:
The Day and Hour Unknown
“No one knows about that day or hour, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
NIV.33: Be on guard! Be alert! You do not know when that time will come.
NIV.34: It’s like a man going away: He leaves his house and puts his servants in charge, each with his assigned task, and tells the one at the door to keep watch.
NIV.35: “Therefore keep watch because you do not know when the owner of the house will come back—whether in the evening, or at midnight, or when the rooster crows, or at dawn.
NIV.36: If he comes suddenly, do not let him find you sleeping.
NIV.37: What I say to you, I say to everyone: ‘Watch!’”

NRSVCE.1: As he came out of the temple, one of his disciples said to him, “Look, Teacher, what large stones and what large buildings!”
NRSVCE.2: Then Jesus asked him, “Do you see these great buildings? Not one stone will be left here upon another; all will be thrown down.”
NRSVCE.3: When he was sitting on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately,
NRSVCE.4: “Tell us, when will this be, and what will be the sign that all these things are about to be accomplished?”
NRSVCE.5: Then Jesus began to say to them, “Beware that no one leads you astray.
NRSVCE.6: Many will come in my name and say, ‘I am he!’ and they will lead many astray.
NRSVCE.7: When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, do not be alarmed; this must take place, but the end is still to come.
NRSVCE.8: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; there will be earthquakes in various places; there will be famines. This is but the beginning of the birth pangs.
NRSVCE.9: “As for yourselves, beware; for they will hand you over to councils; and you will be beaten in synagogues; and you will stand before governors and kings because of me, as a testimony to them.
NRSVCE.10: And the good news must first be proclaimed to all nations.
NRSVCE.11: When they bring you to trial and hand you over, do not worry beforehand about what you are to say; but say whatever is given you at that time, for it is not you who speak, but the Holy Spirit.
NRSVCE.12: Brother will betray brother to death, and a father his child, and children will rise against parents and have them put to death;
NRSVCE.13: and you will be hated by all because of my name. But the one who endures to the end will be saved.
NRSVCE.14: “But when you see the desolating sacrilege set up where it ought not to be (let the reader understand), then those in Judea must flee to the mountains;
NRSVCE.15: the one on the housetop must not go down or enter the house to take anything away;
NRSVCE.16: the one in the field must not turn back to get a coat.
NRSVCE.17: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing infants in those days!
NRSVCE.18: Pray that it may not be in winter.
NRSVCE.19: For in those days there will be suffering, such as has not been from the beginning of the creation that God created until now, no, and never will be.
NRSVCE.20: And if the Lord had not cut short those days, no one would be saved; but for the sake of the elect, whom he chose, he has cut short those days.
NRSVCE.21: And if anyone says to you at that time, ‘Look! Here is the Messiah!’ or ‘Look! There he is!’ — do not believe it.
NRSVCE.22: False messiahs and false prophets will appear and produce signs and omens, to lead astray, if possible, the elect.
NRSVCE.23: But be alert; I have already told you everything.
NRSVCE.24:
“But in those days, after that suffering, the sun will be darkened,
and the moon will not give its light,
NRSVCE.25:
and the stars will be falling from heaven,
and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.
NRSVCE.26: Then they will see ‘the Son of Man coming in clouds’ with great power and glory.
NRSVCE.27: Then he will send out the angels, and gather his elect from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of heaven.
NRSVCE.28: “From the fig tree learn its lesson: as soon as its branch becomes tender and puts forth its leaves, you know that summer is near.
NRSVCE.29: So also, when you see these things taking place, you know that he is near, at the very gates.
NRSVCE.30: Truly I tell you, this generation will not pass away until all these things have taken place.
NRSVCE.31: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
NRSVCE.32: “But about that day or hour no one knows, neither the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
NRSVCE.33: Beware, keep alert; for you do not know when the time will come.
NRSVCE.34: It is like a man going on a journey, when he leaves home and puts his slaves in charge, each with his work, and commands the doorkeeper to be on the watch.
NRSVCE.35: Therefore, keep awake — for you do not know when the master of the house will come, in the evening, or at midnight, or at cockcrow, or at dawn,
NRSVCE.36: or else he may find you asleep when he comes suddenly.
NRSVCE.37: And what I say to you I say to all: Keep awake.”

NET.1: Now as Jesus was going out of the temple courts, one of his disciples said to him, “Teacher, look at these tremendous stones and buildings!”
NET.2: Jesus said to him, “Do you see these great buildings? Not one stone will be left on another. All will be torn down!”
NET.3: So while he was sitting on the Mount of Olives opposite the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked him privately,
NET.4: “Tell us, when will these things happen? And what will be the sign that all these things are about to take place?”
NET.5: Jesus began to say to them, “Watch out that no one misleads you.
NET.6: Many will come in my name, saying, ‘I am he,’ and they will mislead many.
NET.7: When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, do not be alarmed. These things must happen, but the end is still to come.
NET.8: For nation will rise up in arms against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places, and there will be famines. These are but the beginning of birth pains.
NET.9: “You must watch out for yourselves. You will be handed over to councils and beaten in the synagogues. You will stand before governors and kings because of me, as a witness to them.
NET.10: First the gospel must be preached to all nations.
NET.11: When they arrest you and hand you over for trial, do not worry about what to speak. But say whatever is given you at that time, for it is not you speaking, but the Holy Spirit.
NET.12: Brother will hand over brother to death, and a father his child. Children will rise against parents and have them put to death.
NET.13: You will be hated by everyone because of my name. But the one who endures to the end will be saved.
NET.14: “But when you see the abomination of desolation standing where it should not be (let the reader understand), then those in Judea must flee to the mountains.
NET.15: The one on the roof must not come down or go inside to take anything out of his house.
NET.16: The one in the field must not turn back to get his cloak.
NET.17: Woe to those who are pregnant and to those who are nursing their babies in those days!
NET.18: Pray that it may not be in winter.
NET.19: For in those days there will be suffering unlike anything that has happened from the beginning of the creation that God created until now, or ever will happen.
NET.20: And if the Lord had not cut short those days, no one would be saved. But because of the elect, whom he chose, he has cut them short.
NET.21: Then if anyone says to you, ‘Look, here is the Christ!’ or ‘Look, there he is!’ do not believe him.
NET.22: For false messiahs and false prophets will appear and perform signs and wonders to deceive, if possible, the elect.
NET.23: Be careful! I have told you everything ahead of time.
NET.24: “But in those days, after that suffering, the sun will be darkened and the moon will not give its light;
NET.25: the stars will be falling from heaven, and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.
NET.26: Then everyone will see the Son of Man arriving in the clouds with great power and glory.
NET.27: Then he will send angels and they will gather his elect from the four winds, from the ends of the earth to the ends of heaven.
NET.28: “Learn this parable from the fig tree: Whenever its branch becomes tender and puts out its leaves, you know that summer is near.
NET.29: So also you, when you see these things happening, know that he is near, right at the door.
NET.30: I tell you the truth, this generation will not pass away until all these things take place.
NET.31: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will never pass away.
NET.32: “But as for that day or hour no one knows it — neither the angels in heaven, nor the Son — except the Father.
NET.33: Watch out! Stay alert! For you do not know when the time will come.
NET.34: It is like a man going on a journey. He left his house and put his slaves in charge, assigning to each his work, and commanded the doorkeeper to stay alert.
NET.35: Stay alert, then, because you do not know when the owner of the house will return — whether during evening, at midnight, when the rooster crows, or at dawn —
NET.36: or else he might find you asleep when he returns suddenly.
NET.37: What I say to you I say to everyone: Stay alert!”
